result.chapter_summary: In this preface, Hirsch argues that shared background knowledge is the functional core of 'American ethnicity,' which enables the rapid and efficient communication necessary for a modern nation. He contends that literacy is synonymous with this shared ethnicity and that the primary duty of American schools is to transmit this knowledge to ensure national unity and high literacy levels.

claims.text: A 'cult of ethnicity' has arisen among both non-Anglo whites and nonwhite minorities to denounce assimilation and promote separate racial communities.
claims.source_passage: A cult of ethnicity has arisen both among non-Anglo whites and among nonwhite minorities to denounce the goal of assimilation, to challenge the concept of “one people,” and to protect, promote, and perpetuate separate ethnic and racial communities.

claims.text: Shared background knowledge is the defining characteristic of an ethnicity that enables rapid and effective communication within a society.
claims.source_passage: It illustrates how the shared background knowledge that makes up an ethnicity enables rapid and effective communication in a society.

claims.text: Efficiency of communication has been favored by evolution and is a universal characteristic of all human tribes and modern societies.
claims.source_passage: Efficiency of communication has been favored by evolution. It characterizes all human tribes and all modern societies.

claims.text: Accurate interpretation of language depends upon both the speaker and listener sharing a significant amount of unstated knowledge.
claims.source_passage: It is a universal characteristic of evolved human language that an accurate interpretation of what is said depends upon both speaker and listener sharing a lot of unstated knowledge.

claims.text: The thirty-percent reading gap between black and white students is entirely overcome in schools that teach a coherent American-ethnicity curriculum.
claims.source_passage: This book will show that this thirty-percent gap is entirely overcome in schools that teach a coherent American-ethnicity curriculum.

claims.text: The central purpose of the modern elementary school is to impart a national ethnicity to every young citizen.
claims.source_passage: Historians of the modern world have shown that to impart to every young citizen the national ethnicity is the central purpose of the modern elementary school.

claims.text: The social use of language depends as much on world and cultural knowledge as it does on knowledge of the language itself.
claims.source_passage: The social use of language depends as much on that knowledge as it does on knowledge of language itself.

claims.text: Acquiring American literacy depends upon gaining American ethnicity.
claims.source_passage: Acquiring American literacy depends upon gaining American ethnicity.

claims.text: It is a chief duty of the American elementary school to impart American ethnicity to all its students.
claims.source_passage: it is a chief duty of the American elementary school to impart American ethnicity to all its students.

claims.text: Ethnicity is learned and not inborn, allowing a person to learn more than one ethnicity.
claims.source_passage: Ethnicity is not inborn; it is learned. And one can easily learn more than one ethnicity.

claims.text: America’s declining literacy is explained by the increasing failure to impart American literate background knowledge in schools.
claims.source_passage: Our increasing failure to impart American literate background knowledge in our schools explains America’s declining literacy.

claims.text: High literacy levels join people together, while low and uneven literacy levels separate them and contribute to national disunity.
claims.source_passage: Universal high literacy joins people together. Low and uneven literacy separates them. When America’s patriotic sentiment is weaker than its party allegiance it’s past time to raise our literacy levels!

claims.text: Literacy alone confers usability, dignity, and self-respect on industrial man.
claims.source_passage: alone confers usability and dignity and self-respect on industrial man.

claims.text: In every modern nation, the national literacy level is dependent upon citizens possessing in common a strong shared national ethnicity.
claims.source_passage: In every modern nation, the national literacy level is dependent upon citizens possessing in common a strong shared national ethnicity.

claims.text: The failure to impart shared background knowledge is contributing to current American disunity and bipolarity.
claims.source_passage: It is contributing to our current disunity and bipolarity.

claims.text: When national patriotic sentiment is weaker than party allegiance, it indicates a pressing need to raise literacy levels.
claims.source_passage: When America’s patriotic sentiment is weaker than its party allegiance it’s past time to raise our literacy levels!

claims.text: The decline of patriotism and unity in America is powerfully connected to the developmental, child-centered individualism of early education.
claims.source_passage: This book, American Ethnicity, shows that a decline of patriotism and unity in America is powerfully connected to the developmental, child-centered individualism of our early education.

claims.text: American education took a significant turn toward individualism ('I') rather than communalism ('WE') around the middle of the 20th century.
claims.source_passage: It took a big turn to the “I” rather than the “We” around the middle of the 20th century.

claims.text: Sharing a common ethnicity throughout a nation is more important to its unity than having a common national language.
claims.source_passage: sharing a common ethnicity throughout a nation is even more important to its unity than having a common national language!

claims.text: Effective communication and social fellowship between Americans depend on the possession of widely shared background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: A person’s ability to communicate effectively with other Americans (and to feel fellowship with them) depends on the possession of widely shared background knowledge.

claims.text: In human speech globally, a significant amount of meaning always remains unstated.
claims.source_passage: In human speech over the world, much always remains unstated.

claims.text: Speed and precision of communication, as well as fellowship and allegiance, have been evolutionary advantages for human survival.
claims.source_passage: Speed and precision of communication (plus the sense of fellowship and allegiance) have been advantageous for human survival.

claims.text: An American adult lacking literate background knowledge is less likely to be wealthy.
claims.source_passage: An American child who grows into adulthood lacking the literate background knowledge required for effective communication in speech and writing is... less likely to be wealthy.

claims.text: Teenage scores on the Armed Forces Qualification Test (AFQT) are a valid measure of mastery of American ethnicity.
claims.source_passage: He looked at one measure of a mastery of American ethnicity – teenage scores on the Armed Forces Qualification Test

claims.text: There is a linear relationship between higher teenage literacy scores and higher adult income.
claims.source_passage: the chart shows a linear relationship between higher teen literacy scores and later adult income.

claims.text: The United States will soon no longer be dominated by any single ancestral ethnicity or race.
claims.source_passage: In a few years, the USA will no longer be dominated by any single ancestral ethnicity or race or skin color

claims.text: The contents of American ethnicity will change over time to include more elements from non-Anglo cultures.
claims.source_passage: The specific details of its contents will be changed by history and by popular demand – with inclusion of more elements from non-Anglo cultures.

claims.text: Disputes over the specific details of a multicultural curriculum must not distract from the urgent need to improve literacy and equality.
claims.source_passage: But we must not allow disputes over the details of non-Anglo inclusions to divert attention away from the pressing need to improve our literacy-competence and our equality.

claims.text: Capitalizing skin color terms like 'black' and 'white' can unwittingly reinforce racial essentialism.
claims.source_passage: Professor Wilton and her colleagues rightly point out that capitalizing unwittingly essentializes blackness

claims.text: Essentialism is the core component of racism.
claims.source_passage: Wilton and her colleagues argue (rightly in my view) that essentialism is the very essence of racism

claims.text: American literacy scores were higher in the past because schools deliberately attempted to 'Americanize' all students.
claims.source_passage: We used to score higher in literacy than we do now, because our schools used to try very deliberately to “Americanize” all students.

claims.text: The connection between literacy and income should move educational policy out of the 'culture wars' and into the realm of popular state action.
claims.source_passage: the connection between literacy and income ought to remove the issue from abstract culture wars and into forthright, popularly approved actions by our state legislatures and state officials.

claims.text: Curricula must be both multicultural and 'American-ized' to ensure inclusivity while enabling effective communication.
claims.source_passage: They need both to multicultural-ize and to American-ize the curriculum in a way that makes every group feel included, but that also enables Americans to understand books and lectures and newspapers and be able to communicate effectively with other literate Americans.

claims.text: Low-income parents recognize that literacy is the foundation of wealth and seek schools that teach 'American literate ethnicity.'
claims.source_passage: Many astute low-income parents emphatically recognize the literacy-wealth connection. They try hard to place their children in schools that impart American literate ethnicity.

claims.text: American literacy has declined because public schools adopted 'wrong theories' and lost sight of the ethnic foundation of literacy.
claims.source_passage: Because our public schools have (through wrong theories) lost sight of the ethnic foundation of literacy, our literacy has declined.

claims.text: The SAT Verbal scores of the highest-performing students declined significantly between 1952 and 2012.
claims.source_passage: Here is a chart of the literacy scores of our best and brightest (those who take the Scholastic Achievement Test to gain admission to college) – from 1952 to 2012.

claims.text: Public schools should be required to impart literate American ethnicity, particularly for students who do not receive it at home.
claims.source_passage: we should require our public schools also to impart literate American ethnicity – especially for the sake of those who do not acquire it at home.

claims.text: Shared background knowledge is a prerequisite for basic classroom communication and understanding the language of instruction.
claims.source_passage: Shared background knowledge is an essential part of reading, writing, and arithmetic – and even of communication within the classroom itself, where all students need to understand the language of instruction.

claims.text: The theory of 'natural development' currently dominates American schools and is the primary cause of educational decline.
claims.source_passage: To foster this kind of improvement we will need to abandon a wrong theory that currently dominates our schools and that has precipitated our educational decline – the theory of natural “development.”

claims.text: Brain studies and cognitive psychology indicate that the 'natural development' model of student-led discovery is scientifically unsupported.
claims.source_passage: Scientists have recently determined that this natural-development idea is not supported by the actual nature of the human child nor by the character of the human brain.

claims.text: Human education is a process where 'tribal elders' imprint shared mores and ethnicity onto a child's neocortex.
claims.source_passage: Studies in cognitive psychology and brain function say that human education is conducted by tribal elders who imprint upon the neocortex of the child the shared mores and knowledge of the tribe – its ethnicity.

claims.text: The persistent black-white literacy gap is an 'educational scandal' exacerbated by the rise of developmentalism.
claims.source_passage: the educational scandal of our persistent black-white literacy gap... the nation’s downward trend of SAT scores from 1950 to the present and the recent halt in progress for black literacy completely covers the period of the takeover of our schools by developmentalism.

claims.text: Developmentalism became dominant in schools at the same time as racial desegregation, preventing black students from fully benefiting from educational equality.
claims.source_passage: For, unfortunately, developmentalism became dominant during the very period when blacks gained greater equality through school desegregation.

claims.text: The black-white literacy gap is entirely overcome in schools that implement a coherent American-ethnicity curriculum.
claims.source_passage: This book will show that this thirty-percent gap is entirely overcome in schools that teach a coherent American-ethnicity curriculum.

claims.text: Introducing the shared-ethnicity principle improves both the level and the fairness of school results across all types of schools.
claims.source_passage: In every American elementary school – regular, private, and charter – in which the shared-ethnicity principle has been introduced, the level and the fairness of school results have vastly improved.

claims.text: The rise of developmentalism explains why black reading scores reached a standstill after initial progress.
claims.source_passage: This rise of developmentalism explains a fact that has puzzled the Educational Testing Service – makers of the SAT – who report that that after initial progress, black reading scores came to a standstill.

claims.text: Over the past thirty years, black eighth graders have scored thirty percentage points behind white eighth graders in reading.
claims.source_passage: Over the past thirty years, black eighth graders have scored in reading behind white eighth graders by thirty percentage points.

claims.text: Eighty percent of white thirteen-year-olds score higher in reading comprehension than fifty percent of black thirteen-year-olds.
claims.source_passage: That is to say: eighty percent of white thirteen -year-old boys and girls score higher in reading compre�hension than fifty percent of black thirteen-year-old boys and girls.

claims.text: Education schools repudiate the successful results of shared-ethnicity curricula because they contradict dominant natural-development theory.
claims.source_passage: These facts remain scandalously unacknowledged and even repudiated by our education schools which teach and certify our teachers, because it contravenes the dominant, natural-development theory.

claims.text: Teachers and principals in practice prioritize successful results over educational theories.
claims.source_passage: But teachers and principals in the trenches care about results more than about theories.

claims.text: Systematic knowledge building is making a comeback in American schools.
claims.source_passage: Systematic knowledge building is making a comeback because American ethnicity is a reality.

claims.text: The content of shared background knowledge is partially historically determined and partially subject to contemporary change.
claims.source_passage: Some of that knowledge is historically de�termined, as with all long-standing print cultures in any language. But a lot of its content changes with the times and is fully up for grabs.

claims.text: The doctrines of developmentalism are nation-weakening and scientifically incorrect.
claims.source_passage: I invite my colleagues in our education schools to accept what science is saying in their neighboring science departments about the incorrect, nation-weakening doctrines of developmentalism.

claims.text: Parents should systematically intervene to overcome declines in literacy and community.
claims.source_passage: I invite parents to get into the fray, not just when some issue related to sexual activity or race arises, but more systematically to help overcome our declines in literacy and community.

claims.text: The instructional slogan 'guide on the side' is based on a false analogy between physical development and mental socialization.
claims.source_passage: They should know that our prospective teacher is instructed to be a “guide on the side, not a sage on the stage.” That brilliant slogan depends upon a false analogy between the natural development of the human body and the schooling and socialization of the human mind.

claims.text: The child’s neocortex is a blank slate that requires instruction from outside rather than internal development.
claims.source_passage: The child’s neocortex is a blank slate. It doesn’t “develop.” It gets instruction from outside – if not from the sage on the stage – then from influencers outside of the classroom.

claims.text: State legislatures and officials have a duty to create specific curricular guides.
claims.source_passage: Finally, to those legally constituted legislatures and state officials who have a duty to create curricular guides, I say: “Attend to your duties. Get specific.”

claims.text: The United States currently ranks twenty-fifth among thirty-five nations on the Program for International Student Assessment (PISA).
claims.source_passage: Right now, our rank on the Program for International Student Assessment is twenty-fifth among thirty-five nations.

claims.text: A German-speaking Swiss citizen can communicate more effectively with a French-speaking Swiss citizen than with a German-speaking Austrian.
claims.source_passage: The German-speaking editor stated that he could communicate better with a French-speaking Swiss than with a fellow-German-speaking Austrian.

claims.text: The black home environment in the United States has been historically less equipped to compensate for the failures of elementary schools because of the legacy of enforced illiteracy during slavery.
claims.source_passage: Because of our history, when we enforced illiteracy upon slaves, blacks have had fewer home resources to counteract mistaken school theories. The black home environment has been less able to make up for the deficiencies of the elementary school.

claims.text: The specific contents of national ethnicity are not fixed but are subject to change through history and popular demand.
claims.source_passage: The specific details of its contents will be changed by history and by popular demand – with inclusion of more elements from non-Anglo cultures. ... a lot of its content changes with the times and is fully up for grabs.

claims.text: Proper schooling and socialization of the young human mind requires the presence of a 'sage on the stage' rather than a mere guide.
claims.source_passage: The young mind requires a sage on the stage. The child’s neocortex is a blank slate. It doesn’t “develop.” It gets instruction from outside.

claims.text: When party allegiance becomes stronger than patriotic sentiment within a nation, it serves as a critical indicator that literacy levels must be raised.
claims.source_passage: When America’s patriotic sentiment is weaker than its party allegiance it’s past time to raise our literacy levels!

claims.text: Communication in children develops as a constellation of knowledge and skills reflecting their interaction with the world and other cultures, rather than just linguistic knowledge.
claims.source_passage: What we see developing so slowly in our studies of children’s communication is a constellation of knowledge and skills that reflects the child’s interaction with the world and with other people and cultures.

evidence.description: A field experiment in Harvard Square comparing communication length between 'natives' and 'tourists' seeking directions.
evidence.source_reference: Social and Non-Social Speech, Scientific American, Feb. 1977
evidence.source_passage: A typical one goes, “Yes, well you go down on the subway... That’s five words versus eighty words.

evidence.description: The observation that understanding unstated information is a universal requirement for accurate linguistic interpretation.
evidence.source_reference: Daniel T. Willingham, Why Don't Students Like School?, 2nd ed., 2021
evidence.source_passage: Professor Daniel Willingham of the University of Virginia has explained why this is so in his aphorism: “A reading test is a knowledge test in disguise.”

evidence.description: The historical claim that modern industrial society requires school-transmitted culture rather than folk culture.
evidence.source_passage: “A school-transmitted culture, not a folk-transmitted one, alone confers usability and dignity and self-respect on industrial man.”

evidence.description: The analysis of American historical trends moving from communal identity to individualism.
evidence.source_passage: traces our modern American story “From I to We and Back to I” and about how we need to get to “WE” again.

evidence.description: Karl Deutsch's study of Switzerland showing national unity across four languages via shared background knowledge.
Program for International Student Assessment (PISA) rankings where the US ranks 25th out of 35 nations.
Philip Cohen's analysis plotting teenage AFQT scores against adult income, showing a linear correlation.
evidence.source_reference: Cohen, Philip (2014) 'How well do teen test scores predict adult income?'

evidence.description: Chart showing the decline of SAT Verbal scores by decade from 1952 to 2012.
Richard Alba's research on the shifting racial and ethnic demographics of the United States.
evidence.source_reference: Alba, Richard, The Great Demographic Illusion

evidence.description: 25,000 mothers in the South Bronx annually apply for spots in seven Core Knowledge public schools.
Studies in cognitive psychology and brain function regarding how education works as tribal imprinting.
An ETS report stating that black reading scores came to a standstill after initial progress.
evidence.source_reference: Barton & Coley, The Black-White Achievement Gap: When Progress Stopped (2010)

evidence.description: NAEP data showing eighth-grade black students score 30 percentage points behind white students in reading.
Educational Testing Service (ETS) report on the stagnation of the black-white achievement gap since 1990.
evidence.source_reference: Barton, Paul E.; Coley, Richard J., The Black-White Achievement Gap: When Progress Stopped. Policy Information Report, Educational Testing Service, 2010
evidence.source_passage: after initial progress, black reading scores came to a standstill.11 Currently black literacy lags far behind white literacy.

evidence.description: Comparative reading comprehension scores of white and black 13-year-olds showing a 30-percentage point gap.
evidence.source_reference: Not explicitly cited in text, likely NAEP via ETS report
evidence.source_passage: eighty percent of white thirteen -year-old boys and girls score higher in reading compre�hension than fifty percent of black thirteen-year-old boys and girls.

evidence.description: The commercial version of the Core Knowledge Language Arts Program grossed $100 million in one year, indicating market demand for knowledge-based curricula.
evidence.source_passage: I just learned that the commercial version of this program grossed a hundred million dollars in sales this current year.

concepts.author_definition: A functional, learned 'communicative store' of shared background knowledge that allows strangers to communicate efficiently.
concepts.common_alternative: A racial or genealogical heritage that is inborn.
concepts.source_passage: Ethnicity is not inborn; it is learned... I use a purely functional, descriptive label for American Ethnicity. I name it “the communicative store.”

concepts.author_definition: The possession of enough shared background knowledge to understand books, newspapers, and lectures addressed to a general audience.
concepts.common_alternative: The formal skill of decoding letters and words.
concepts.source_passage: Some people share enough background knowledge to communicate readily in writing with strangers who are also literate. That means that they can understand books and newspapers and lectures addressed to a general audience.

concepts.author_definition: A term used by Karl Deutsch to describe the shared store of information that allows members of a group to communicate effectively.
concepts.source_passage: “Shared communicative store” comes close to the term Karl Deutsch uses in his Nationalism and Social Communication: “communicative complementarity.”

concepts.author_definition: The belief that certain characteristics (like skin color or ethnicity) are part of a person's essential, unchangeable nature; regarded by the author as the essence of racism.
concepts.source_passage: Wilton and her colleagues rightly point out that capitalizing unwittingly essentializes blackness; it implies that skin color is by implication part of a person’s essential nature... essentialism is the very essence of racism

concepts.author_definition: The educational theory, often associated with Dewey, that children should follow their own instincts and discover knowledge on their own rather than through direct instruction.
concepts.source_passage: the theory of natural “development.” Current brain studies show that the “natural” development of the young human is not fostered when she is encouraged to go her own way in choosing her own books or by making her own discoveries

concepts.author_definition: A metaphorical term for teachers and adult educators who pass down the shared mores and knowledge of the community to children.
concepts.source_passage: human education is conducted by tribal elders who imprint upon the neocortex of the child the shared mores and knowledge of the tribe – its ethnicity.

concepts.author_definition: The biological claim that the child's mind does not possess an internal blueprint for academic development but must receive 'intelligent instruction from the elders of the tribe.'
concepts.common_alternative: The idea of the mind as having 'innate developmental stages.'
concepts.source_passage: The child’s neocortex is a blank slate. It doesn’t “develop.” It gets instruction from outside.

concepts.author_definition: An instructor who provides direct, intelligent instruction from the 'elders of the tribe' to the child's neocortex, contrasted with a passive 'guide on the side.'
A formal culture imparted by educational institutions that provides the usability, dignity, and self-respect required for individuals in an industrial society.
Local or ancestral cultural knowledge passed down informally, which the author argues is insufficient on its own for success in modern industrial society.
The specific set of shared background knowledge required for a student to comprehend the books and instruction appropriate for the third-grade level.

cases.name: The Harvard Square Direction Experiment
cases.description: A researcher used a hidden tape recorder to compare the brevity of directions given to a perceived local versus a self-identified tourist. It found that communication with a native took 5 words ('First stop on the subway'), while explaining the same process to a tourist took 80 words, demonstrating the massive efficiency gain provided by shared background knowledge.
key_passages: The passersby, thinking they are addressing a fellow Bostonian, don’t even break their stride when they give their replies, which consist of a few words like “First stop on the subway.”
This time the tapes show that people’s answers are much longer and more rudimentary... That’s five words versus eighty words.

cases.name: Switzerland
cases.description: A multilingual nation that maintains high levels of patriotism and internal communication because citizens share a national ethnicity (background knowledge) despite speaking four different languages.
key_passages: One of his striking examples was Switzerland, a nation that is both unified and fiercely patriotic yet speaks and writes four different national languages.
The German-speaking editor stated that he could communicate better with a French-speaking Swiss than with a fellow-German-speaking Austrian. That’s ethnicity at work!

cases.name: Core Knowledge public schools in the Bronx
cases.description: Seven public schools in the South Bronx that use the Core Knowledge Sequence to teach American ethnicity, seeing massive demand from local low-income parents (25,000 applicants for kindergarten).
key_passages: each year, twenty-five thousand mothers in the low-income South Bronx try to place their child in the kindergarten of one of the seven Core Knowledge public schools in the Bronx.

cases.name: US Educational Decline (1952-2012)
cases.description: A sixty-year period marked by the decline of SAT Verbal scores among top students, which the author attributes to the abandonment of knowledge-based schooling.
key_passages: Here is a chart of the literacy scores of our best and brightest (those who take the Scholastic Achievement Test to gain admission to college) – from 1952 to 2012.

cases.name: Core Knowledge Language Arts Program (CKLA)
cases.description: A specific literacy curriculum that successfully implements the shared-ethnicity principle. It has seen massive commercial success ($100 million in sales), suggesting a shift in the educational market toward knowledge-based instruction.
key_passages: Core Knowledge Language Arts Program created by the Core Knowledge Foundation is a literacy program that tries to help impart American eth�nicity in a systematic way.
I just learned that the commercial version of this program grossed a hundred million dollars in sales this current year.
contestable_aspects: The causal link between the curriculum and specific score gains
The characterization of the program as imparting 'ethnicity'

cases.name: American Education Schools
cases.description: Institutions responsible for teacher certification that, according to Hirsch, systematically ignore evidence of success for knowledge-based curricula because such evidence contradicts their ideological commitment to developmentalism.
key_passages: These facts remain scandalously unacknowledged and even repudiated by our education schools which teach and certify our teachers, because it contravenes the dominant, natural-development theory.
contestable_aspects: Whether education schools 'repudiate' the facts or simply interpret 'fairness' and 'results' differently

thinkers.name: Arthur M. Schlesinger, Jr.
thinkers.key_idea: The rise of a 'cult of ethnicity' threatens American assimilation and unity.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Schlesinger's critique of the 'cult of ethnicity' as a jumping-off point to redefine ethnicity as a functional necessity for communication rather than a divisive racial category.
key_passages: A cult of ethnicity has arisen both among non-Anglo whites and among nonwhite minorities to denounce the goal of assimilation

thinkers.name: Ernest Gellner
thinkers.key_idea: Industrial man requires a school-transmitted culture to achieve usability and dignity.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites Gellner to support the claim that the fundamental role of modern elementary schools is to transmit a national culture/ethnicity.
key_passages: A school-transmitted culture, not a folk-transmitted one, alone confers usability and dignity and self-respect on industrial man.

thinkers.name: Daniel Willingham
thinkers.key_idea: Reading tests are knowledge tests in disguise.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch adopts Willingham's psychological insight and extends it into a sociological/nationalist claim about ethnicity.
key_passages: “A reading test is a knowledge test in disguise.” That insight can be pressed further: An American reading test is an American ethnicity test in disguise.

thinkers.name: Robert Putnam
thinkers.key_idea: The narrative of modern America is a transition 'From I to We and Back to I'.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch positions his book as a specific 'chapter' in Putnam's larger story of American social trends.
key_passages: This book about our decline in literacy and allegiance is a chapter in the wider story told by Robert Putnam in his illuminating 2020 book The Upswing

thinkers.name: Karl Deutsch
thinkers.key_idea: National unity depends on 'communicative complementarity' (shared ethnicity) more than a shared language.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Deutsch's theoretical framework to define 'ethnicity' in a functional, communicative sense.
key_passages: The great historian Karl Deutsch showed that sharing a common ethnicity throughout a nation is even more important to its unity than having a common national language!

thinkers.name: Leigh Wilton
thinkers.key_idea: Multiculturalism can increase race essentialism, which is a scientific mistake and a driver of racism.
thinkers.source_work: Valuing Differences and Reinforcing Them: Multiculturalism Increases Race Essentialism
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites Wilton to explain why he does not capitalize 'black' and 'white', arguing against essentializing racial categories.
key_passages: Professor Wilton and her colleagues rightly point out that capitalizing unwittingly essentializes blackness... essentialism is the very essence of racism

thinkers.name: John Dewey
thinkers.key_idea: Children should make their own discoveries through 'instincts and powers.'
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch argues that Dewey's idea of natural development is contradicted by modern neuroscience and cognitive psychology.
key_passages: by making her own discoveries through exercising her own “instincts and powers” (Dewey’s phrase).

thinkers.name: Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
thinkers.key_idea: The appropriate use of capitalization in racial and organizational names (e.g., 'The Black Church' vs. 'black').
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch adopts Gates's convention of not capitalizing skin colors to avoid essentializing race.
key_passages: this book follows the lead of Henry Louis Gates, Jr. in his The Black Church... it’s desirable not to capitalize skin color.

actors.name: Arthur M. Schlesinger, Jr.
actors.context: Historian who critiqued the decline of American unity and the rise of multiculturalism.

actors.name: Daniel Willingham
actors.context: Cognitive scientist who emphasizes the role of knowledge in reading.

actors.name: Ernest Gellner
actors.context: Scholar of nationalism who argued schools are the source of modern national culture.

actors.name: Robert Putnam
actors.context: Sociologist who wrote about the decline of the 'WE' in American life.

actors.name: Philip Cohen
actors.context: Professor at the University of Maryland who researched the link between AFQT scores and income.

actors.name: Richard Alba
actors.context: Scholar who documents the decline of white dominance in American demographics.

actors.name: Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
actors.context: Scholar whose style choices regarding the capitalization of 'black' Hirsch follows.

actors.name: Core Knowledge Foundation
actors.context: Provides a downloadable Sequence of topics to impart American ethnicity.

actors.name: Educational Testing Service (ETS)
actors.context: Makers of the SAT who reported the standstill in black reading scores.

actors.name: Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
actors.context: Cited regarding the house style for capitalizing 'Black' and 'White' in his work 'The Black Church'.

actors.name: Educational Testing Service (ETS)
actors.context: Makers of the SAT who tracked the stagnation of reading scores among black students.

actors.name: Core Knowledge Foundation
actors.context: Non-profit organization that develops the knowledge-based curriculum Hirsch advocates.

actors.name: State Legislatures
actors.context: Entities charged with the legal duty to create specific curricular guides for schools.

objections_raised.objection: Disputes over the inclusion of non-Anglo cultural elements in the shared curriculum.
objections_raised.source: Unattributed (refers to popular demand and academic debate)
objections_raised.response: The curriculum should and will change to be more inclusive, but these disputes over details must not distract from the primary goal of achieving high literacy for all.

objections_raised.objection: The idea of 'Americanizing' students is an out-of-fashion or problematic concept tied to culture wars.
objections_raised.response: The pragmatic connection between literacy and income, along with the ability to 'multicultural-ize' the curriculum, makes it a matter of fairness and inclusion rather than just a culture war issue.

objections_raised.objection: Education schools repudiate the 'facts' regarding the success of knowledge-based curricula.
objections_raised.response: Teachers and principals 'in the trenches' care more about results than these ideologies and are moving ahead with knowledge-based programs.

objections_raised.objection: Teachers should be 'guides on the side' rather than 'sages on the stage.'
objections_raised.response: This is a false analogy based on physical growth; the mind is a blank slate that requires external instruction.

cross_chapter_refs.reference_text: My first chapter reveals that each year, twenty-five thousand mothers in the low-income South Bronx try to place their child in the kindergarten...
That’s a theme of Chapter 1, where the advantages of getting specific are illustrated.
